prompt:
Middleware-team ticket. Shipping an iflow to our SAP Cloud Integration tenant is currently: export zip, click through the Design UI, press Deploy, stare at the Monitor tab. Twice this quarter someone deployed an OLDER bundle over a hotfix because nothing checks what's already on the tenant. Build the deployer we keep talking about: a small Python package cpideploy/ (stdlib only, python3, no pip installs) that lists what's there, uploads a new version, kicks off deployment, and follows it to a definitive started-or-failed answer. test_cpi_deploy.py is the acceptance contract; it and everything under docs/ are protected.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official SAP documentation for the Cloud Integration OData API (Integration Content) — the Integration Content overview (which resource manages design-time artifacts, which one runtime artifacts, and the CSRF ritual every modifying call needs), the integration-flow example-requests page (how a draft is updated, what ArtifactContent must contain, and how a draft becomes a numbered version — mind the quoting style in those query parameters), the documented three-call deploy sequence (deploy action -> build/deploy status by task -> runtime artifact status), and the two status pages so you know which build and runtime values are terminal and which mean keep-waiting. First-party sources only: help.sap.com / SAP's official SAP-docs GitHub mirror / api.sap.com. docs/contract.json pins the wire contract our mock speaks, docs/official_sources.json the provenance; if live docs contradict a pin, report the conflict in your summary and leave the protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests import (internal layout beyond this is yours, 3+ modules expected):
- cpideploy.client.CpiClient(base_url, user, password) — basic auth on every request, JSON via $format=json, and the documented token dance for modifying calls: fetch once the way SAP describes, cache token AND session cookie, attach both to every write; plain reads must not trigger the pre-flight. Non-2xx responses with an OData error document raise cpideploy.client.CpiApiError exposing status, code, message.value in str(e), and the untouched error object (error_body) — SAP's diagnostic members ride along verbatim. Credentials never appear in any exception text.
- cpideploy.artifacts.list_versions(client, artifact_id) — the documented $filter query for one artifact's design-time versions, returned oldest-to-newest. Careful: our version strings are dotted numerics and '1.10.0' is NEWER than '1.9.0'; string sort corrupts the ordering the conflict check depends on.
- cpideploy.artifacts.upload_new_version(client, artifact_id, name, version, content: bytes) — refuse to touch the tenant if any existing version is the same or newer (raise VersionConflictError naming the blocking version, zero modifying requests on the wire — the mock counts). Otherwise: update the draft with the base64-encoded bundle exactly as the docs show (byte-exact round trip; watch out for base64 line-wrapping), then save it as the target version.
- cpideploy.artifacts.deploy_and_wait(client, artifact_id, version, wait, max_polls) — trigger the documented deploy action (its response body hands you the task id), poll the build/deploy status resource until a terminal value (anything non-terminal: call the injected wait(seconds) — there is no time.sleep anywhere in this package), then read the runtime artifact status until it's final. Return {'task_id', 'build_status', 'runtime_status'}. A failed build raises DeploymentError naming the status and must not query runtime state; a runtime ERROR raises DeploymentError carrying the detail read from the documented error-information $value resource; a status that never goes terminal within max_polls raises DeploymentError naming the task id.

Acceptance: python3 test_cpi_deploy.py — must print the final OK line. Loopback mock only, dummy credentials, no sleeps, no wall-clock reads.

research.product: SAP Cloud Integration (SAP Integration Suite) — OData API, Integration Content
research.interface: Integration Content API 1.0.0 at <tenant>/api/v1 (OData 2.0): IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts, IntegrationDesigntimeArtifactSaveAsVersion, DeployIntegrationDesigntimeArtifact, BuildAndDeployStatus, IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts

official_sources.title: Integration Content | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror of help.sap.com)
official_sources.used_for: IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts resource (read/download/create/upload/deploy/delete integration flows); IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts (read/deploy/undeploy, error information); BuildAndDeployStatus resource; CSRF flow for modifying actions: GET with header X-CSRF-Token: Fetch, token returned in the X-CSRF-Token response header, then sent on the modifying request; service root <host>/api/v1

official_sources.title: Integration Flow Example Requests | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: PUT IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts(Id='...',Version='active') with body {Name, ArtifactContent}; ArtifactContent is the base64-encoded content of the integration flow bundle (zip); POST IntegrationDesigntimeArtifactSaveAsVersion?Id='...'&SaveAsVersion='...' with single-quoted parameters

official_sources.title: Get Runtime Status of Deployed Integration Flow | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: documented three-call sequence: POST /DeployIntegrationDesigntimeArtifact?Id='<bundleId>'&Version='<version>' returns the taskId; GET /BuildAndDeployStatus(TaskId='<taskid>') returns the build status; on Success, GET /IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts('<bundleId>') returns the runtime Status

official_sources.title: Build and Deploy Status | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: build/deploy status values SUCCESS, FAIL, DEPLOYING, FAIL_ON_LICENSE_ERROR and their semantics

official_sources.title: Runtime Status | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: runtime status values: Started and Error are final states, Starting and Stopping are intermediate

official_sources.title: Runtime Artifacts and Error Information Example Requests | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: GET IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts('<id>')/ErrorInformation and .../ErrorInformation/$value returning the error information in JSON format

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Integration Content (Version 1.0.0)
official_sources.used_for: API name/version (IntegrationContent 1.0.0), 'read, update, deploy, or undeploy integration artifacts', 'implemented based on OData version 2 specification', service URL shape .../api/v1

research.language_rationale: The release tooling this deployer joins is Python; SAP documents the Integration Content API as plain OData 2.0 over HTTP (basic/OAuth auth, JSON bodies, base64 ArtifactContent) and ships no supported Python SDK for it, so urllib from the stdlib against the documented wire contract is the credible dependency-free surface.
